reasoning: EIF1AD is a component of the translation initiation machinery that assists in the formation of the 43S pre‑initiation complex. Reducing EIF1AD levels by CRISPRi is expected to impair general protein synthesis, which can lead to accumulation of nascent polypeptides in the endoplasmic reticulum and trigger the unfolded protein response (UPR). A hallmark of the UPR is the transcriptional up‑regulation of ER‑resident chaperones such as Hsp90b1 (GRP94) to restore proteostasis. Because there is no known direct regulatory link between EIF1AD and Hsp90b1, the most likely effect is an indirect stress‑induced increase in Hsp90b1 expression.
